```python
target = "101010101010101"
assert target in counts, f"Target {target} not found in results"

# Check that the target is the most frequent result
target_count = counts.get(target, 0)
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    if bitstring != target:
        assert target_count > count, f"Target {target} is not the clearly amplified solution"

# Check that the observed probability is reasonably close to expected (e.g., within 10% margin)
observed_probability = target_count / shots
assert abs(observed_probability - 0.42) < 0.1, f"Observed probability {observed_probability} deviates from expected 0.42"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
assert target in counts, "Target bitstring missing from results"
# Verify that the target bitstring is indeed the most frequent outcome
max_count = max(counts.values())
assert counts[target] == max_count, f"Target {target} is not the most frequent: {counts[target]} vs {max_count}"
# Check that the target probability is significant (above 30%, accounting for sampling noise)
assert counts[target] / shots > 0.3, f"Target probability too low: {counts[target] / shots}"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
assert target in counts, f"Target {target} not found in results"

# Check if target is the most frequent result and exceeds its expected probability threshold
# Using a 10% margin on the expected 0.42 probability to account for sampling noise
target_prob = counts.get(target, 0) / shots
assert target_prob > 0.30, f"Target probability {target_prob} is too low"

# Check if target is significantly more frequent than any other bitstring
max_other = max([v for k, v in counts.items() if k != target] + [0]) / shots
assert target_prob > max_other, "Target is not the most frequent bitstring"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
assert target in counts, f"Target bitstring {target} not found in counts"

target_count = counts.get(target, 0)
other_counts = {k: v for k, v in counts.items() if k != target}

# Ensure target is the most frequent result
max_other_count = max(other_counts.values()) if other_counts else 0
assert target_count > max_other_count, f"Target count {target_count} not the highest (max other: {max_other_count})"

# Ensure target probability is significantly higher than any distractor (~0.42 vs <0.1)
target_prob = target_count / shots
assert target_prob > 0.3, f"Target probability {target_prob} is too low"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
assert target in counts, f"Target {target} not found in results"

# Check if target is the most frequent result and exceeds a reasonable threshold
target_count = counts[target]
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_bitstring == target, f"Target {target} was not the most frequent, got {most_frequent_bitstring}"

# Verify the target count is statistically significant (approx 42% of shots)
target_proportion = target_count / shots
assert 0.35 <= target_proportion <= 0.50, f"Target proportion {target_proportion} outside expected range"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
```
```python
# Check if the target bitstring has the highest count
top_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert top_bitstring == target, f"Expected {target} to be the most frequent, but got {top_bitstring}"

# Check if the target is significantly more frequent than the next best (a distractor)
target_count = counts.get(target, 0)
remaining_counts = [count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != target]
max_distractor_count = max(remaining_counts) if remaining_counts else 0
assert target_count > max_distractor_count * 1.5, "Target bitstring is not clearly amplified"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
```
```python
assert target in counts, "Target bitstring missing from results"
assert counts[target] > 0.35, f"Target bitstring probability too low: {counts[target]}"

# Check that the target is the most frequent result
max_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert max_bitstring == target, f"Target {target} was not the most frequent, found {max_bitstring}"

# Verify distractor probabilities are lower than the target
for bitstring, prob in {"101010101010100": 0.09, "101010101010111": 0.08}.items():
    if bitstring in counts:
        assert counts[bitstring] < counts[target], f"Distractor {bitstring} exceeded target"
```

```python
# The target bitstring should have the highest frequency count
target = "101010101010101"
assert counts.get(target, 0) > max(
    [count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != target], 
    default=0
)

# Verify the measured probability is reasonably close to the theoretical target probability (42%)
target_measured_prob = counts.get(target, 0) / shots
assert abs(target_measured_prob - 0.42) < 0.1
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
assert target in counts, f"Target {target} not found in results"

# Check that the target is the most frequent result and significantly amplified
target_count = counts.get(target, 0)
max_other_count = max([c for b, c in counts.items() if b != target] or [0])

assert target_count > max_other_count, f"Target count {target_count} not the highest (max distractor: {max_other_count})"
assert (target_count / shots) > 0.3, f"Target probability {target_count / shots} too low"
```

```python
target = "101010101010101"
# Verify that the target bitstring is the most frequent result
max_count_key = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert max_count_key == target, f"Expected {target} as most frequent, but got {max_count_key}"

# Verify the target count is significantly higher than others (roughly 42% of shots)
target_count = counts.get(target, 0)
assert target_count / shots > 0.35, f"Target count fraction {target_count/shots} is too low"

# Verify distractor counts are lower than target
for distractor, prob in {"101010101010100": 0.09, "101010101010111": 0.08}.items():
    assert counts.get(distractor, 0) < target_count, f"Distractor {distractor} is not less frequent than target"
```
